puzzle.title: The Village on the Shores of Trial
puzzle.intro: You find yourself in a coastal village, where the sound of waves mixes with whispers of a forgotten people. They were given a command of rest, but their greed led to a punishment that is still spoken of in hushed tones. Can you uncover who they were?
clues: We lived by the shore and were given a command to observe a day of rest.
On that day, fish would come to us in abundance, but we were forbidden to take them.
Some among us warned against transgression, but others conspired to fish anyway.
We violated the sacred command and brought upon ourselves a severe punishment.
Our punishment was a transformation that left us disfigured and disgraced.
We were turned into apes, a sign for all who would come after.
puzzle.arabic: وَسْـَٔلْهُمْ عَنِ ٱلْقَرْيَةِ ٱلَّتِى كَانَتْ حَاضِرَةَ ٱلْبَحْرِ إِذْ يَعْدُونَ فِى ٱلسَّبْتِ إِذْ تَأْتِيهِمْ حِيتَانُهُمْ يَوْمَ سَبْتِهِمْ شُرَّعًۭا وَيَوْمَ لَا يَسْبِتُونَ ۙ لَا تَأْتِيهِمْ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ نَبْلُوهُم بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَفْسُقُونَ
puzzle.verse: And ask them about the town that was by the sea - when they transgressed in [the matter of] the sabbath - when their fish came to them openly on their sabbath day, and the day they had no sabbath they did not come to them. Thus did We give them trial because they were defiantly disobedient. (7:163)
